content.text:
Please analyze this codebase and create a CLAUDE.md file, which will be given to future instances of Claude Code to operate in this repository.

What to add:
1. Commands that will be commonly used, such as how to build, lint, and run tests. Include the necessary commands to develop in this codebase, such as how to run a single test.
2. High-level code architecture and structure so that future instances can be productive more quickly. Focus on the "big picture" architecture that requires reading multiple files to understand.

Usage notes:
- If there's already a CLAUDE.md, suggest improvements to it.
- When you make the initial CLAUDE.md, do not repeat yourself and do not include obvious instructions like "Provide helpful error messages to users", "Write unit tests for all new utilities", "Never include sensitive information (API keys, tokens) in code or commits".
- Avoid listing every component or file structure that can be easily discovered.
- Don't include generic development practices.
- If there are Cursor rules (in .cursor/rules/ or .cursorrules) or Copilot rules (in .github/copilot-instructions.md), make sure to include the important parts.
- If there is a README.md, make sure to include the important parts.
- Do not make up information such as "Common Development Tasks", "Tips for Development", "Support and Documentation" unless this is expressly included in other files that you read.
- Be sure to prefix the file with the following text:

```
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
```

file.content:
#!/bin/sh
# Zweck: Manuelle Synchronisierung der Projektdefinitionen nach projects/registry.js.
# Quelle: projects/*/meta.js

set -eu

ROOT_DIR=$(pwd)
PROJECTS_DIR="$ROOT_DIR/projects"
REGISTRY_PATH="$PROJECTS_DIR/registry.js"
TMP_FILE=$(mktemp)

printf '%s\n' '/**' > "$TMP_FILE"
printf '%s\n' ' * Zweck: Zentrale Projektdefinitionen (generiert).' >> "$TMP_FILE"
printf '%s\n' ' * Quelle: Projekte mit meta.js; Änderungen an dieser Datei werden beim Sync überschrieben.' >> "$TMP_FILE"
printf '%s\n' ' */' >> "$TMP_FILE"
printf '\n' >> "$TMP_FILE"
printf '%s\n' 'export const projectRegistry = [' >> "$TMP_FILE"

COUNT=0
LANDING_COUNT=0
ROUTES=""

for DIR in "$PROJECTS_DIR"/*; do
  [ -d "$DIR" ] || continue

  ID=$(basename "$DIR")
  case "$ID" in
    .* ) continue ;;
  esac

  META_PATH="$DIR/meta.js"
  [ -f "$META_PATH" ] || continue

  ROUTE=$(sed -En "s/^[[:space:]]*route:[[:space:]]*'([^']*)'.*/\\1/p" "$META_PATH" | head -n1)
  LANDING=$(sed -En "s/^[[:space:]]*isLanding:[[:space:]]*(true|false).*/\\1/p" "$META_PATH" | head -n1)
  TITLE=$(sed -En "s/^[[:space:]]*title:[[:space:]]*'([^']*)'.*/\\1/p" "$META_PATH" | head -n1)

  if [ -z "$ROUTE" ]; then
    echo "Missing route in $META_PATH" >&2
    rm -f "$TMP_FILE"
    exit 1
  fi

  if [ -z "$LANDING" ]; then
    echo "Missing isLanding in $META_PATH" >&2
    rm -f "$TMP_FILE"
    exit 1
  fi

  if [ -z "$TITLE" ]; then
    TITLE="$ID"
  fi

  printf '%s\n' "$ROUTES" | grep -qx "$ROUTE" && {
    echo "Duplicate route detected: $ROUTE" >&2
    rm -f "$TMP_FILE"
    exit 1
  }
  ROUTES=$(printf '%s\n%s' "$ROUTES" "$ROUTE")

  [ "$LANDING" = "true" ] && LANDING_COUNT=$((LANDING_COUNT + 1))

  printf '%s\n' '  {' >> "$TMP_FILE"
  printf "    id: '%s',\n" "$ID" >> "$TMP_FILE"
  printf "    route: '%s',\n" "$ROUTE" >> "$TMP_FILE"
  printf "    isLanding: %s,\n" "$LANDING" >> "$TMP_FILE"
  printf "    title: '%s',\n" "$TITLE" >> "$TMP_FILE"
  printf "    loader: () => import('./%s/index.js'),\n" "$ID" >> "$TMP_FILE"
  printf '%s\n' '  },' >> "$TMP_FILE"

  COUNT=$((COUNT + 1))
done

if [ "$LANDING_COUNT" -ne 1 ]; then
  echo "Exactly one landing project required, found: $LANDING_COUNT" >&2
  rm -f "$TMP_FILE"
  exit 1
fi

printf '%s\n' '];' >> "$TMP_FILE"
printf '\n' >> "$TMP_FILE"
printf '%s\n' 'export function getProjectRegistry() {' >> "$TMP_FILE"
printf '%s\n' '  return projectRegistry;' >> "$TMP_FILE"
printf '%s\n' '}' >> "$TMP_FILE"

mv "$TMP_FILE" "$REGISTRY_PATH"
echo "Synced $COUNT project(s) -> projects/registry.js"

input.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

Landing page for Gnomon Practice (gnomonpractice.com). A vanilla JavaScript single-page app with a canvas-based animated pixel raster visualization. No build tools, no bundler, no framework — served directly as static files via ES modules.

## Development

Open `index.html` in a browser (or use any static file server). There is no build step, no package manager, and no test suite.

### Registry Sync

After adding or modifying a project in `projects/`, run:

```sh
sh scripts/sync-projects-registry.sh
```

This regenerates `projects/registry.js` from each project's `meta.js`. Do not edit `registry.js` by hand — it will be overwritten.

## Architecture

### App Bootstrap

`index.html` → `app.js` → loads the landing project via the registry → calls `mount()`.

`app.js` also sets up a sigmoid-based font scale controller that updates `--font-scale-factor` CSS variable on window resize.

### Project System

Projects live in `projects/<id>/` with this structure:
- **`meta.js`** — route, title, isLanding flag (read by sync script)
- **`index.js`** — must export `mount({ appRoot, project })` and `unmount()`
- **`controller.js`** — state and simulation logic
- **`view.js`** / **`controls.js`** — rendering and UI (currently placeholder stubs)

`projects/registry.js` is the generated central registry. Exactly one project must have `isLanding: true`.

### Landing Project (`projects/_landing/`)

Full-screen canvas rendering a circular dot raster with wind/noise-driven animation:
- **`controller.js`** — `createRasterController()` manages the pixel grid, vector field, 3D noise, and animation loop via `requestAnimationFrame`. Exposes setters for wind direction, noise influence, turbulence, wind strength, transfer rate, and pixel smoothing.
- **`index.js`** — `mount()` builds the DOM (canvas, start/pause toggle, vertical sliders, about toggle), wires controls to the controller. `unmount()` tears everything down.

### Shared Utilities (`lib.js`)

`createToggleButton()` — reusable DOM toggle button with state management.
`sigmoid()` / `calculateSigmoidFactor()` — used by the font scale controller.

## Conventions

- German JSDoc headers describe each file's purpose (`Zweck:`) and scope.
- All DOM construction is imperative (no templates, no JSX). Styles are set via `element.style`.
- Code uses ES modules with explicit `.js` extensions in imports.
- No external dependencies.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

Landing page for Gnomon Practice (gnomonpractice.com). A vanilla JavaScript single-page app with a canvas-based animated pixel raster visualization. No build tools, no bundler, no framework — served directly as static files via ES modules.

## Development

Open `index.html` in a browser (or use any static file server). There is no build step, no package manager, and no test suite.

### Registry Sync

After adding or modifying a project in `projects/`, run:

```sh
sh scripts/sync-projects-registry.sh
```

This regenerates `projects/registry.js` from each project's `meta.js`. Do not edit `registry.js` by hand — it will be overwritten.

## Architecture

### App Bootstrap

`index.html` → `app.js` → loads the landing project via the registry → calls `mount()`.

`app.js` also sets up a sigmoid-based font scale controller that updates `--font-scale-factor` CSS variable on window resize.

### Project System

Projects live in `projects/<id>/` with this structure:
- **`meta.js`** — route, title, isLanding flag (read by sync script)
- **`index.js`** — must export `mount({ appRoot, project })` and `unmount()`
- **`controller.js`** — state and simulation logic
- **`view.js`** / **`controls.js`** — rendering and UI (currently placeholder stubs)

`projects/registry.js` is the generated central registry. Exactly one project must have `isLanding: true`.

### Landing Project (`projects/_landing/`)

Full-screen canvas rendering a circular dot raster with wind/noise-driven animation:
- **`controller.js`** — `createRasterController()` manages the pixel grid, vector field, 3D noise, and animation loop via `requestAnimationFrame`. Exposes setters for wind direction, noise influence, turbulence, wind strength, transfer rate, and pixel smoothing.
- **`index.js`** — `mount()` builds the DOM (canvas, start/pause toggle, vertical sliders, about toggle), wires controls to the controller. `unmount()` tears everything down.

### Shared Utilities (`lib.js`)

`createToggleButton()` — reusable DOM toggle button with state management.
`sigmoid()` / `calculateSigmoidFactor()` — used by the font scale controller.

## Conventions

- German JSDoc headers describe each file's purpose (`Zweck:`) and scope.
- All DOM construction is imperative (no templates, no JSX). Styles are set via `element.style`.
- Code uses ES modules with explicit `.js` extensions in imports.
- No external dependencies.

- **Project overview** — vanilla JS static site, no build tools
- **Development** — how to run (just open `index.html`), registry sync script
- **Architecture** — app bootstrap flow, project system structure, landing project internals, shared utilities
- **Conventions** — German JSDoc headers, imperative DOM construction, ES modules with `.js` extensions, no dependencies

content.text:
The issue is that `open index.html` opens the file via `file://` protocol, and browsers block ES module imports (`type="module"`) from `file://` URLs due to CORS restrictions.

You need a local HTTP server. The simplest option:

```sh
python3 -m http.server 8000
```

Then open `http://localhost:8000` in your browser.

Want me to start that for you?

Checking the proxy and the firewall
ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED
